capability_claim: Trace-grounded reverse engineering of unfamiliar deterministic byte protocols from limited examples.

closest_existing_benchmarks.name: SWE-bench
closest_existing_benchmarks.relationship: Both reward practical software reasoning, but this benchmark is trace inference rather than repository issue repair.

closest_existing_benchmarks.name: LiveCodeBench
closest_existing_benchmarks.relationship: Both use objective executable-style scoring, but this benchmark is not contest programming and has no natural-language algorithm statement.

closest_existing_benchmarks.name: BIG-bench inductive reasoning tasks
closest_existing_benchmarks.relationship: Both include rule induction, but this package uses generated byte-level protocol traces with exact JSONL scoring and an isolated solver bundle.

task_boundary.allowed_tools: Any local computation, notes, scripts, or symbolic search that only reads solver_bundle.
task_boundary.forbidden_tools: Inspecting private files outside solver_bundle, including generator, scorer, verifier, gold, audit traces, validation reports, hidden seeds, or solution labels.

item_generation.public_dev_subset: Regenerate with arbitrary seeds using generator.py for development.
item_generation.provenance: Synthetic deterministic firmware-like transformations generated locally.
item_generation.contamination_control: Fresh seeded generation, no web-sourced items, no natural-language puzzle corpus, isolated solver bundle.

grading.ambiguity_policy: Only exact 8-hex answers are valid; malformed, missing, duplicate, and extra ids are reported.

human_baseline_plan.qualified_humans: Software engineers with byte/hex and scripting experience.
human_baseline_plan.budget: 90 minutes for 30 items with local computation.

frontier_prescreen_plan.budget: Same solver bundle and time/tool boundary as humans.
frontier_prescreen_plan.pass_condition: Best frontier solver below 50% of qualified human performance while instructions remain clear and verifier passes.
